channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Israeli Knesset member backs settlement push in southern Syria
item.description: An Israeli Knesset member has publicly backed efforts to establish Israeli settlements in southern Syria.

item.title: Man City chair Al Mubarak will ‘say everything’ after verdict on finances
item.description: English Premier League side Manchester City charged with 115 alleged breaches of financial rules.

item.title: ‘Red meat is a dream’: Iran inflation hits highest level since World War II
item.description: The war with the US is not the only factor driving prices skywards.

item.title: At least 49 people die of thirst after truck breakdown in Niger desert
item.description: Two people managed to survive after walking more than 50km to a water source and then a nearby town.

item.title: Barcelona teenager Lamine Yamal wins La Liga Player of the Season award
item.description: Lamine Yamal will be an integral part of Spain's World Cup 2026 challenge and was key in Barcelona's La Liga defence.

item.title: WHO, Africa CDC unveil $518m Ebola plan as Uganda death toll rises
item.description: Uganda reported three more cases and one more death from the outbreak which has spread from the DRC.

item.title: WFP: War on Iran pushing millions towards hunger
item.description: The US-Israeli war on Iran could tip up to 45 million people into acute hunger, the UN's WFP warns.

item.title: NASA tells ISS astronauts to ready for possible evacuation amid leak repair
item.description: NASA says four astronauts sheltering in transport capsule out of 'abundance of caution' as Roscosmos makes repairs.

item.title: Are the US and Iran closer to war or to a deal?
item.description: As US and Iran pursue talks, both signal they are still ready to use force against each other.

item.title: Gaza, Iran, Lebanon: If ceasefires are in place, why do strikes continue?
item.description: International law sets rules for ceasefires, but who enforces them when violations continue? Three experts explain.

item.title: FIFA cancels World Cup tickets for about 60 fans who got them for free
item.description: Mispriced tickets were sold through the official World Cup site ahead of next week's showpiece event for FIFA.

item.title: Israel maintains attacks despite US-brokered deal with Lebanon
item.description: The Israeli army also issues new forced displacement orders for several towns and villages in southern Lebanon.

item.title: Celtic fans oppose Keane as potential manager after Israel role
item.description: Ireland's record goalscorer Robbie Keane played for Celtic but his link to manager's job is facing criticism.

item.title: Iran footballers hand passports to US embassy for World Cup visa approval
item.description: Iran will fly to Mexico on Saturday to their home base at 2026 World Cup, while all their games are played in the US.

item.title: Fujimori vs Sanchez: What to know about Peru’s presidential run-off election
item.description: The run-off follows a tumultuous first round of voting, which saw long lines, delayed results and accusations of fraud.

item.title: Ukrainian marine drone explodes at Romanian port
item.description: A malfunctioning Ukrainian naval drone has caused an explosion at a key Black Sea port in Romania.

item.title: How the US naval blockade has bled Iran of nearly $6bn in oil revenues
item.description: Iran is exporting less than one-sixth of the oil it was shipping before the war began.

item.title: Drone explosion in Romanian port spurs Ukraine war spillover fears
item.description: European Commission president warns that the maritime drone attack was a 'direct consequence' of the Russia-Ukraine war.

item.title: Inside Syria’s fight against the Captagon trade
item.description: Anti-narcotics units in Syria raid homes and seize pills as the government fights back against the Captagon trade.

item.title: Nazi salutes, Sikhs abused: Tensions grip UK after Henry Nowak’s murder
item.description: Although the victim's father has warned against exploiting his family's tragedy, far-right groups are rallying.

item.title: Mexico World Cup 2026 preview: Players to watch, group matches and squad
item.description: El Tri lost in the last 16 in seven consecutive World Cups but have higher hopes for the 2026 tournament.

item.title: South Korean police break up 35-hour polling station protest
item.description: South Korean police break up 35-hour polling station protest

item.title: UN boosts Lebanon aid appeal as Israeli war drives humanitarian crisis
item.description: The UN says it needs nearly $640m to help Lebanon over the next six months.

item.title: Hamas says won’t surrender arms but only police will carry weapons in Gaza
item.description: Hamas official Husam Badran, in an exclusive interview, lays out what the group is ready to do ahead of Cairo talks.

item.title: Shakira and Burna Boy opening World Cup in Mexico, Katy Perry headlines US
item.description: Katy Perry will front the opening US World Cup game, while Alanis Morissette and Michael Buble will open Canada's games.
